label: claude-proxy
title: claude-proxy
author: E-Claw

requiredVars.description: Claude CLI OAuth token — get it from claude.ai/settings/api

steps:
Repo: https://github.com/timothy-node/openclaw-claude-proxy
Forwards OpenClaw messages to the local Claude CLI (--print mode) via HTTP.
Updated: 2026-03-03

== Prerequisites ==
node v22, npm 10.9.4, python3 3.11+

== Step 1: Install uv ==
  curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
  source ~/.local/bin/env

== Step 2: Install Claude Code ==
  npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
  claude --version  # verify installation

== Step 3: Clone claude-proxy ==
  git clone https://github.com/timothy-node/openclaw-claude-proxy \
    ~/.openclaw/skills/claude-proxy

== Step 4: Create venv ==
  cd ~/.openclaw/skills/claude-proxy
  uv venv && source .venv/bin/activate
  uv pip install -r pyproject.toml

== Step 5: Configure .env ==
  cp .env.example .env
  # Edit .env and set:
  #   CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=sk-ant-oat01-...  (see Env Variables page)
  #   CLAUDE_TIMEOUT_DEFAULT=120

== Step 6: Patch proxy.py ==
  In run_claude(), add: env["IS_SANDBOX"] = "1"
  (bypasses root restriction in container environments)

== Step 7: Start ==
  source ~/.local/bin/env
  cd ~/.openclaw/skills/claude-proxy
  source .venv/bin/activate
  export CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=$CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
  nohup python proxy.py > /tmp/proxy.log 2>&1 &

== Verify ==
  curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/health
  Expected: {"status":"ok"}

== API ==
  GET  /health        Health check
  POST /chat          { "message": "..." }

== Stop ==
  pkill -f proxy.py

Optional: Enable auto-start via systemd
  sudo cp claude-proxy.service /etc/systemd/system/
  sudo systemctl enable claude-proxy

Use at your own risk. Provided as-is, without warranty.

label: x-tweet-fetcher
title: X / Twitter Fetcher
author: ythx-101
steps:
Repo: https://github.com/ythx-101/x-tweet-fetcher
Fetch tweets, comments, timelines, and articles from X/Twitter without login or API keys.
Also supports WeChat article search, tweet discovery, and user profile analysis.
Updated: 2026-03-04

== Prerequisites ==
python3 3.7+

== Step 1: Clone ==
  git clone https://github.com/ythx-101/x-tweet-fetcher \
    ~/.openclaw/skills/x-tweet-fetcher

== Step 2: (Optional) Install advanced deps ==
  # Required only for: comments, timelines, @mention monitoring, Google search
  openclaw plugins install @askjo/camofox-browser
  # OR standalone:
  git clone https://github.com/jo-inc/camofox-browser
  cd camofox-browser && npm install && npm start  # Port 9377

== Usage in bot ==
  # All scripts output structured JSON

  # Fetch a single tweet (no API key needed)
  exec: python3 ~/.openclaw/skills/x-tweet-fetcher/scripts/fetch_tweet.py \
    --url "https://x.com/user/status/123456"

  # Search WeChat articles (no API key needed)
  exec: python3 ~/.openclaw/skills/x-tweet-fetcher/scripts/sogou_wechat.py \
    --keyword "AI Agent" --limit 10 --json

  # Discover tweets by keyword (no API key needed)
  exec: python3 ~/.openclaw/skills/x-tweet-fetcher/scripts/x_discover.py \
    --keywords "AI Agent,LLM tools" --limit 5 --json

  # Fetch Chinese platforms (Weibo/Bilibili/CSDN/WeChat)
  exec: python3 ~/.openclaw/skills/x-tweet-fetcher/scripts/fetch_china.py \
    --url "https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/..."

  # Monitor @mentions (cron-friendly: exit 0=nothing new, 1=new mentions)
  exec: python3 ~/.openclaw/skills/x-tweet-fetcher/scripts/fetch_tweet.py \
    --monitor @username

== No API key required for basic use ==
All scripts output structured JSON by default.
Exit codes: 0=nothing new, 1=new content found, 2=error

Use at your own risk. Provided as-is, without warranty.

label: model-hierarchy
title: Model Hierarchy (Cost Optimizer)
author: zscole
steps:
Repo: https://github.com/zscole/model-hierarchy-skill
Automatically routes tasks to the cheapest AI model that can handle them.
Reduces LLM costs by ~10x (80% routine tasks → cheap tier, 15% moderate, 5% complex).
Updated: 2026-02-16

== Cost Tiers ==
  Routine (80%):  Cheap models   $0.14-0.50/M  (file ops, status, formatting)
  Moderate (15%): Mid models     $1-5/M         (code gen, summaries, drafts)
  Complex (5%):   Premium models $10-75/M       (debugging, architecture)

== Installation: OpenClaw ==
  git clone https://github.com/zscole/model-hierarchy-skill \
    ~/.openclaw/skills/model-hierarchy
  # Gateway picks up SKILL.md automatically on restart
  openclaw gateway restart

== Installation: Claude Code ==
  # Copy the routing rules into your CLAUDE.md:
  cat ~/.openclaw/skills/model-hierarchy/SKILL.md >> ~/your-project/CLAUDE.md

== No API keys required ==
This skill adds routing instructions to your agent.
The agent applies them when choosing which model to call.
Model access must already be configured in your OpenClaw gateway.

== Estimated savings (100K tokens/day) ==
  Pure Opus:     ~$225/month
  Pure Sonnet:   ~$45/month
  With Hierarchy: ~$19/month

Use at your own risk. Provided as-is, without warranty.

label: A2A Toolkit
title: EClaw A2A Toolkit — Official API
author: E-Claw
steps:
EClaw A2A Toolkit — Official Server-Side API
Provides web search, web fetch, inter-entity communication, and mission management.
No installation needed — all endpoints are hosted on https://eclawbot.com.
Updated: 2026-03-10

== Authentication ==
All endpoints require: deviceId + botSecret (from your push context).
Replace DEVICE_ID, BOT_SECRET, ENTITY_ID with your actual values.

== 1. Web Search (no API key needed) ==
Search the web via DuckDuckGo. Rate limit: 10 req/min per device.
  exec: curl -s "https://eclawbot.com/api/bot/web-search?q=YOUR_QUERY&deviceId=DEVICE_ID&botSecret=BOT_SECRET&entityId=ENTITY_ID"
Response: { query, results: [{ title, url, snippet }], resultCount }
Optional params: &limit=8 (max 15)

== 2. Web Fetch (extract page content) ==
Fetch a URL and return clean text. Supports HTML, JSON, plain text.
  exec: curl -s "https://eclawbot.com/api/bot/web-fetch?url=TARGET_URL&deviceId=DEVICE_ID&botSecret=BOT_SECRET&entityId=ENTITY_ID"
Response: { url, contentType, title, content, length, truncated }
Optional params: &maxLength=5000 (max 15000)

== 3. Client Speak (Developer/Owner → Entity) ==
Send a message as the device owner (client) to a specific entity, multiple entities, or all.
Auth: deviceId + deviceSecret (NOT botSecret). This resets bot-to-bot rate limits.
  exec: curl -s -X POST "https://eclawbot.com/api/client/speak" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"deviceId":"DEVICE_ID","deviceSecret":"DEVICE_SECRET","entityId":ENTITY_ID,"text":"YOUR_MESSAGE","source":"client"}'
entityId accepts: number (single), array [0,1,2] (multi), or "all" (broadcast).
Optional: "mediaType": "photo|voice|video|file", "mediaUrl": "URL"
Response: { success, message } or { success, sentCount, results }

== 4. Broadcast to ALL entities ==
Send a message to every other bound entity on the same device.
  exec: curl -s -X POST "https://eclawbot.com/api/entity/broadcast" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"deviceId":"DEVICE_ID","fromEntityId":ENTITY_ID,"botSecret":"BOT_SECRET","text":"YOUR_MESSAGE"}'
Response: { success, sentCount, results: [{ entityId, pushed, mode }] }

== 5. Speak to a specific entity ==
Direct message to one entity on the same device.
  exec: curl -s -X POST "https://eclawbot.com/api/entity/speak-to" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"deviceId":"DEVICE_ID","fromEntityId":ENTITY_ID,"toEntityId":TARGET_ENTITY_ID,"botSecret":"BOT_SECRET","text":"YOUR_MESSAGE"}'

== 6. Cross-device speak (via public code) ==
Message an entity on a different device using its public code.
  exec: curl -s -X POST "https://eclawbot.com/api/entity/cross-speak" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"deviceId":"DEVICE_ID","fromEntityId":ENTITY_ID,"botSecret":"BOT_SECRET","targetCode":"PUBLIC_CODE","text":"YOUR_MESSAGE"}'

== 6b. Client cross-speak (no botSecret needed) ==
Send cross-device message as device owner (web portal). No botSecret required.
  exec: curl -s -X POST "https://eclawbot.com/api/client/cross-speak" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"deviceId":"DEVICE_ID","fromEntityId":ENTITY_ID,"targetCode":"PUBLIC_CODE","text":"YOUR_MESSAGE"}'

== 7. Mission Dashboard ==
Read all tasks/notes/rules:
  exec: curl -s "https://eclawbot.com/api/mission/dashboard?deviceId=DEVICE_ID&botSecret=BOT_SECRET&entityId=ENTITY_ID"
Add TODO:
  exec: curl -s -X POST "https://eclawbot.com/api/mission/todo/add" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"deviceId":"DEVICE_ID","entityId":ENTITY_ID,"botSecret":"BOT_SECRET","title":"TITLE","description":"DESC"}'
Mark TODO done:
  exec: curl -s -X POST "https://eclawbot.com/api/mission/todo/done" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"deviceId":"DEVICE_ID","entityId":ENTITY_ID,"botSecret":"BOT_SECRET","title":"TITLE"}'
Add note:
  exec: curl -s -X POST "https://eclawbot.com/api/mission/note/add" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"deviceId":"DEVICE_ID","entityId":ENTITY_ID,"botSecret":"BOT_SECRET","title":"TITLE","content":"CONTENT"}'

== Rate Limits ==
  Web search/fetch: 10 requests per minute per device
  Bot-to-bot messages: 8 consecutive before human intervention required
  Cross-device messages: 4 consecutive before human intervention required
  Broadcast dedup: Same content blocked within 60 seconds

== Media Attachments ==
Broadcast and speak-to support media:
  Add to JSON body: "mediaType": "photo|voice|video|file", "mediaUrl": "URL"

Provided by EClaw Official. Server-hosted — no installation required.

label: search-skills
title: Deep Search Skills
author: blessonism

requiredVars.description: Brave Search API key — free tier at api.search.brave.com (2,000 queries/month free)
(Optional) Exa API key — exa.ai, improves search quality for semantic queries
(Optional) Tavily API key — tavily.com, optimized for AI research tasks

steps:
Repo: https://github.com/blessonism/openclaw-search-skills
Multi-source search + content extraction skills. Three skills included:
  - search-layer:     Parallel search across Brave/Exa/Tavily/Grok, intent-aware scoring, dedup
  - content-extract:  URL → clean Markdown (auto-fallback to MinerU for anti-scrape sites)
  - mineru-extract:   PDF/Office/HTML → Markdown via MinerU API
Updated: 2026-03-05

== Prerequisites ==
python3 3.9+

== Step 1: Clone ==
  git clone https://github.com/blessonism/openclaw-search-skills \
    ~/.openclaw/skills/search-skills

== Step 2: Install dependencies ==
  cd ~/.openclaw/skills/search-skills
  pip install -r requirements.txt

== Step 3: Configure .env ==
  cp .env.example .env
  # Edit .env and set (see Env Variables page):
  #   BRAVE_API_KEY=BSA...   (required — free tier: 2,000 queries/month)
  #   EXA_API_KEY=exa-...    (optional, improves semantic search)
  #   TAVILY_API_KEY=tvly-...  (optional, better for research tasks)

== Step 4: Start ==
  nohup python3 server.py > /tmp/search-skills.log 2>&1 &

== Verify ==
  curl 'http://127.0.0.1:8769/health'
  Expected: {"status":"ok"}

== API ==
  POST /search         { "query": "..." }                 Multi-source search
  POST /extract        { "url": "https://..." }           URL → Markdown
  POST /mineru         { "url": "https://..." }           PDF/Office → Markdown

== Usage in bot ==
  exec: curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8769/search \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    -d '{"query":"YOUR_SEARCH_QUERY"}'

== Stop ==
  pkill -f 'python3 server.py'

Use at your own risk. Provided as-is, without warranty.
